title: Intermediaries, Hop-by-Hop Fields, Forwarding, and Transformation · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first intermediary forwarding and transformation contract for intermediaries, hop-by-hop fields, forwarding, and transformation. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of intermediary forwarding and transformation contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.
Model downstream and upstream connections, framing, versions, deadlines, addresses, TLS, and failures as independent protocol hops.
Update framing, validators, digests, content codings, warnings, and signatures when an intermediary transforms content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Hop By Hop Removal
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 2034 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Hop By Hop Removal.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for remove connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Hop By Hop Removal, keep Hop By Hop Removal connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Intermediary Failure Ownership, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop protocol team handles case 2042 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Intermediary Failure Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Every failure observed by a proxy should be reported as an origin status 500.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Intermediary Failure Ownership happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Intermediary Failure Ownership condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Intermediary Failure Ownership
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Intermediary Failure Ownership: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop protocol team handles case 2043 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Intermediary Failure Ownership.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Intermediary Failure Ownership, keep Intermediary Failure Ownership connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.
